content: Language education vocabulary learning of the term "reflexion" (as a core metacognitive term) can be leveraged to bootstrap more grounded LLM self-reflection mechanisms, by fine-tuning LLMs on high school English learning corpora that embed human intuitive understandings of reflexion as a deliberate step of re-evaluating one’s own output against learning goals. This creates a shared lexical and conceptual anchor that reduces the abstractness of current technical self-reflection frameworks for large language models.
